[[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) shaden.JPG|270px]]
左手前に拝殿、中央奥に本殿。右には八幡社 |所在地 = {{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}拝戸716 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|18|23.67|N|135|59|18.13|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=水尾神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|磐衝別命|Q11587884}}
比咩神 |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}2座）
旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = {{jalink|流造|Q210684}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|5月3日|Q2547}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=水尾神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''水尾神社'''（みおじんじゃ/みをじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|高島市|Q845794}}拝戸（はいど）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。{{jalink|神紋}}は「菊」[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 水尾神社]（滋賀県神社庁）。。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の2柱神社由緒書。。 * '''{{jalink|磐衝別命|Q11587884}}''' （いわつくわけのみこと） *: 第11代{{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}皇子で、『{{jalink|古事記|Q813031}}』『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』では三尾君（三尾氏）・羽咋君（羽咋氏）の祖と記す{{Sfn|水尾神社（神々）|1986年|p=353-355}}。 *: 河南社（南本殿：現在社）の祭神。 * '''比咩神''' （ひめがみ） *: 第26代{{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}の母の{{jalink|振媛|Q28415396}}を指す。 *: 河北社（北本殿）の祭神で、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}46年（{{jalink|1971年|Q2475}}）に河南社に遷座して相殿となり、そののち合祀された。 祭神に関しては、かつて{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦命}}（河南社）・{{jalink|アメノウズメ|Q462803|天鈿女命}}（河北社）の2柱とする説があったが{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}、三尾君の祖神を祀る神社であるとして現在は磐衝別命・比咩神の2柱が祭神に定められている。 === 歴史 === === 創建 ===
'''社伝に基づく祭神略系図'''（史書とは相違） {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{Supra|10}} {{jalink|垂仁天皇|Q314850}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01='''{{jalink|磐衝別命|Q11587884}}'''
（三尾君祖）}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01={{Supra|15}} {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}|02=磐城別王}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01=速総別王|02=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01={{jalink|彦主人王|Q11487892}}|02='''{{color|#FC4E6B|比咩神}}'''
（{{color|#FC4E6B|振姫}}）}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01=天迹部王|02=男迹部王|03=太迹部王
（{{Supra|26}} {{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}）}} {{familytree/end}}
水尾神社は、かつて水尾川を隔てた河南社（こうなみしゃ、南本殿）・河北社（こうほくしゃ、北本殿）の二社から成ったが、現在は河南社の一社から成る。社伝水尾神社の{{jalink|縁起|Q522046}}は、近世の修験者・和邇估容聡（わにこやすとし）によって記された『三尾大明神本土記』ママに基づく {{Harv|水尾神社（神々）|1986年|p=354}}では、{{jalink|磐衝別命|Q11587884}}は{{jalink|サルタヒコ|Q3090037|猿田彦命}}の天成神道を学ぶために猿田彦命を祀る当地に来住したという。そして、朝夕に猿田彦命を祀る三尾大明神（高島市永田の長田神社）を遙拝したのでこの地は「拝所（のち拝戸<はいど>）」と称され、その住居跡は「拝所御所」と称されたと伝える。磐衝別命は当地で亡くなったため、その子・磐城別王は磐衝別命を三尾山に埋葬し、磐衝別命を祀る社として「河南社（現在社）」を創建したという。 続いて社伝によると、{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}の第十一皇子・速総別王も天成神道を学ぶため当地に来住し、その4世孫である{{jalink|彦主人王|Q11487892}}は、磐城別王5世孫の振姫を妃に迎えた。そして振姫は水尾神社の拝殿を産所として、天迹部王（あまあとべ）・男迹部王（おとあとべ）・太迹部王（おおあとべ：第26代{{jalink|継体天皇|Q349277}}を指す）の3子を同時に出産したという。その出産時、彦主人王は仮社を建てて安産を祈ったといい、のち天迹部王がその仮社跡に両親を祀る三重生大明神（みえなり-）を創建したのが「河北社」の始まりで、その後同社は比咩神（振姫）のみを祀るようになったと伝える。この河北社は昭和46年（1971年）に倒壊したため、現在は河南社（現在地）に遷座している。 === 概史 === 文献の初見は『{{jalink|新抄格勅符抄|Q11502312}}』『新抄格勅符抄』10（神事諸家封戸） 大同元年（806年）牒。で、{{jalink|天平神護|Q1373181}}元年（{{jalink|765年|Q31216}}）に近江国の「三尾神」に対して13戸の{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}（神社付属の民戸）が給されている{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。その後、いずれも「三尾神」に対して{{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}3年（{{jalink|784年|Q30083}}）に従五位下、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}5年（{{jalink|863年|Q25052}}）に従四位下の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられた記事が見える{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|近江国|Q843882}}{{jalink|高島郡 (滋賀県)|Q11669692|高島郡}}に「水尾神社二座 並名神大 月次新嘗」として、二座が{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}で{{jalink|幣帛|Q11481885}}に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。 {{jalink|弘安|Q389180}}3年（{{jalink|1280年|Q5536}}）に禅智院が開かれると同院は水尾神社の{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}となったという{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。その後は振るわず社勢は衰微した。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、明治15年（{{jalink|1882年|Q7812}}）に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}4年（{{jalink|1915年|Q2084}}）に{{jalink|県社}}に列格した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|延暦|Q1373176}}3年（{{jalink|784年|Q30083}}）{{jalink|8月3日|Q2786}}、従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本紀|Q611399}}』） - 表記は「三尾神」。 * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}5年（{{jalink|863年|Q25052}}）閏{{jalink|6月27日|Q2664}}、正五位下から従四位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「三尾神」。 === 境内 === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿・中門}}]] 本殿は一間社{{jalink|流造|Q210684}}。本殿前には中門を挟んで拝殿が建てられている。拝殿は{{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}}。また境内には{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}8年（{{jalink|1996年|Q2070}}）に和風庭園の「水尾庭園」が完成した。 そのほか、境内裏手の山中には{{jalink|6世紀|Q8090}}の群集墳（拝戸古墳群）が残っており、うち皇子塚は三尾君祖の墳墓であると伝える。 File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|水尾庭園 === 摂末社 === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|八幡社}}]] * 八幡社（3社相殿）摂末社の記載は神社由緒書による。 - 3社は独立社であったが明治44年（{{jalink|1911年|Q2076}}）に1殿に併祀。 ** 神明神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照皇大神}}、{{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受大神}}。 ** 八幡神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}、速総別命（応神天皇皇子）。 ** 秋葉神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火結命}}。{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}15年（{{jalink|1587年|Q6744}}）に{{jalink|秋葉山本宮秋葉神社|Q500413|秋葉神社}}から勧請。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 （1月1日）年間祭事の記載は神社由緒書による。 * 左義長祭 （1月15日） * 祈年祭 （3月16日） * 鎮火祭 （4月15日） * 例祭 （5月3日） * 泥落祭 （6月初旬） * 百燈祭 （6月26日） * 敬老祭、八幡神社例祭、戦没者慰霊祭 （9月15日） * 秋葉神社例祭 （9月18日） * 秋正月 （10月17日） * 新嘗祭 （11月23日） === 関係地 === * 北本殿跡 ** 所在地：高島市上拝戸（{{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=北本殿跡}}） *: 河北社（北本殿）跡。昭和46年（{{jalink|1971年|Q2475}}）の台風で倒壊したため、河南社（現・水尾神社）に遷座した。跡地には石碑が建てられていたが、それものちに移転したため遺構はない[https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html 水尾神社北本殿（旧地）]（延喜式神社の調査<個人サイト>）。。 * 長田神社 ** 所在地：高島市永田（{{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=長田神社（三尾大明神）}}） *: 式内社。磐衝別命が朝夕拝んだ「三尾大明神」であるという。 * 大炊神社 （おおたきじんじゃ） ** 所在地：高島市音羽（{{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=大炊神社（大炊殿跡か）}}） *: 水尾神社の大炊殿の跡であるという{{Sfn|水尾神社（神々）|1986年|p=353-355}}。また、社頭の大石は水尾神社一の鳥居の礎石であるともいう{{Sfn|水尾神社（神々）|1986年|p=353-355}}。 * 太田神社 ** 所在地：高島市安曇川町青柳（{{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=太田神社（御旅所）}}） *: 旧称「新宮神社」。水尾神社の御旅所であったといい、水尾神社との距離から往時の威勢が指摘される{{Sfn|水尾神社（神々）|1986年|p=353-355}}。 * 三重生神社 （みおうじんじゃ） ** 所在地：高島市安曇川町常磐木（{{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=三重生神社}}） *: 式内社。祭神は彦主人王と振姫。社名「三重生」は振姫の産んだ3子に因むとされる。 * 安産もたれ石 ** 所在地：高島市安曇川町田中（{{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=安産もたれ石（振姫出産伝承地）}}） *: 振姫の3子出産伝承地。 * {{jalink|田中王塚古墳}}（安曇陵墓参考地） ** 所在地：高島市安曇川町田中（{{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=田中王塚古墳（安曇陵墓参考地、彦主人王墓伝承地））}}） *: 彦主人王墓の伝承地。 === 考証 === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|三尾神社（{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|大津市|Q202907}}）}}]] 水尾神社との関係が推測される神社として、{{jalink|滋賀県|Q131358}}{{jalink|大津市|Q202907}}の{{jalink|園城寺|Q1756307}}近くに地主神の{{jalink|三尾神社|Q65247874}}が知られる{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。『輿地志略』によると、同社は霊材に乗って高島郡三尾崎から大津に流れ着いた3神を祀るという{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。このことから、水尾神社との関連が指摘されるとともに、史料に見える「近江国三尾神」がこちらを指す可能性の指摘もある{{Sfn|水尾神社（平凡社）|1991年}}。 Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|May 3rd|Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. {{jalink|Divine Crest}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{jalink|Shinhime|Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{jalink|Ame no Uzume|Q462803|Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===
'''Simplified genealogy of the enshrined deities based on shrine legends'' (different from history books) {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{Supra|10}} {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01='''{{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}}'''
(Mio-kunso)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01={{Supra|15}} {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}|02=Iwaki Betsuo}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01=Sokusobetsuou|02=○}}{{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01={{jalink|Hiko Shushuō|Q11487892}}|02='''{{color|#FC4E6B|Higajin}}'''
（{{color|#FC4E6B|Furihime}})}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01=Tenkabu-o|02=Otokobe-o|03=Tai-kabu-o
({{Supra|26}} {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}})}} {{familytree/end}}
Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{jalink|auspicious origin|Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{jalink|Hikoshuno|Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}), 13 {{jalink|Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}3 years ({{jalink|784|Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{jalink|Kankai|Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Omi Province|Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{jalink|Koan|Q389180}}3 ({{jalink|1280|Q5536}}), it became {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1882|Q7812}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} . === God level === * {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{jalink|784th year|Q30083}}) {{jalink|August 3rd|Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) Leap {{jalink|June 27th|Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{jalink|Nagazuzō|Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{jalink|6th century|Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{jalink|Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|Akiba Shrine}} in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}15 ({{jalink|1587|Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{jalink|Tanakaozuka Tumulus}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{jalink|Mio Shrine|Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West Japan) {{jalink|Kosei Line|Q599974}} {{jalink|Omi-Takashima Station|Q5363194}} (about 35 minutes walk) === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/131}} Refer to 131 frames of "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Naka"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection). * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Tetsuo Hashimoto|year=1986|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 25 Place names in Shiga Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwashibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} * {{jalink|Hiko Master King|Q11487892}} * {{jalink|Hakui Shrine|Q11610064}} === External link === Prefecture|Q130290}}{{jalink|Himeji City|Q209801}}{{jalink|Miojinja in Yamanoi Town}}|Mizuo Shrine (Himeji City)|Mizuo Shrine in Himeji City, Hyogo Prefecture {{jalink|Yasutomi Town|Q8050114}}|Mizuo Shrine (Yasutomi Town, Himeji City)}} {{Shrine |Name = Mizuo Shrine |Image = [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) shaden.JPG|270px]]
The worship hall is in the front left, and the main shrine is in the center back. Hachiman Shrine on the right |Location = {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|35|18|23.67|N|135|59|18.13|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Mizuo Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Iwashibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}}
Himegami |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}}2 seats)
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{jalink|Ryuzo|Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|May 3rd|Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. {{jalink|Divine Crest}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{jalink|Shinhime|Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{jalink|Ame no Uzume|Q462803|Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===
'''Simplified genealogy of the enshrined deities based on shrine legends'' (different from history books) {{familytree/start|style="text-align:center; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01={{Supra|10}} {{jalink|Emperor Suinin|Q314850}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||01| 01='''{{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}}'''
(Mio-kunso)}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01={{Supra|15}} {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}|02=Iwaki Betsuo}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||02| 01=Sokusobetsuou|02=○}}{{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01||||01| 01=○}} {{familytree|border=0|||!||||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02| 01={{jalink|Hiko Shushuō|Q11487892}}|02='''{{color|#FC4E6B|Higajin}}'''
（{{color|#FC4E6B|Furihime}})}} {{familytree|border=0|||,|-|+|-|.}} {{familytree|border=0|01|02|03| 01=Tenkabu-o|02=Otokobe-o|03=Tai-kabu-o
({{Supra|26}} {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}})}} {{familytree/end}}
Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{jalink|auspicious origin|Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{jalink|Hikoshuno|Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}), 13 {{jalink|Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}3 years ({{jalink|784|Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{jalink|Kankai|Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Omi Province|Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{jalink|Koan|Q389180}}3 ({{jalink|1280|Q5536}}), it became {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1882|Q7812}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} . === God level === * {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{jalink|784th year|Q30083}}) {{jalink|August 3rd|Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) Leap {{jalink|June 27th|Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{jalink|Nagazuzō|Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{jalink|6th century|Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{jalink|Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|Akiba Shrine}} in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}15 ({{jalink|1587|Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{jalink|Tanakaozuka Tumulus}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{jalink|Mio Shrine|Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125}} (JR West Japan) {{jalink|Kosei Line|Q599974}} {{jalink|Omi-Takashima Station|Q5363194}} (about 35 minutes walk) === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{jalink|Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/131}} Refer to 131 frames of "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Naka"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection). * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Tetsuo Hashimoto|year=1986|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 25 Place names in Shiga Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Iwashibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} * {{jalink|Hiko Master King|Q11487892}} * {{jalink|Hakui Shrine|Q11610064}} === External link === In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{jalink|Shinhime|Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{jalink|Ame no Uzume|Q462803|Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{jalink|auspicious origin|Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{jalink|Hikoshuno|Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}), 13 {{jalink|Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}3 years ({{jalink|784|Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{jalink|Kankai|Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Omi Province|Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{jalink|Koan|Q389180}}3 ({{jalink|1280|Q5536}}), it became {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1882|Q7812}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} . === God level === * {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{jalink|784th year|Q30083}}) {{jalink|August 3rd|Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) Leap {{jalink|June 27th|Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{jalink|Nagazuzō|Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{jalink|6th century|Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{jalink|Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|Akiba Shrine}} in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}15 ({{jalink|1587|Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{jalink|Tanakaozuka Tumulus}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{jalink|Mio Shrine|Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{jalink|Shinhime|Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{jalink|Ame no Uzume|Q462803|Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{jalink|auspicious origin|Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{jalink|Hikoshuno|Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}), 13 {{jalink|Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}3 years ({{jalink|784|Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{jalink|Kankai|Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Omi Province|Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{jalink|Koan|Q389180}}3 ({{jalink|1280|Q5536}}), it became {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1882|Q7812}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} . === God level === * {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{jalink|784th year|Q30083}}) {{jalink|August 3rd|Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) Leap {{jalink|June 27th|Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{jalink|Nagazuzō|Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{jalink|6th century|Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{jalink|Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|Akiba Shrine}} in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}15 ({{jalink|1587|Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{jalink|Tanakaozuka Tumulus}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{jalink|Mio Shrine|Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{jalink|Shinhime|Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{jalink|Emperor Keitai|Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{jalink|Ame no Uzume|Q462803|Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{jalink|auspicious origin|Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{jalink|Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{jalink|Sartahiko|Q3090037|Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{jalink|Hikoshuno|Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{jalink|Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{jalink|Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181}} ({{jalink|765|Q31216}}), 13 {{jalink|Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}}3 years ({{jalink|784|Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{jalink|Kankai|Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|Extended}} 5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Omi Province|Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{jalink|Koan|Q389180}}3 ({{jalink|1280|Q5536}}), it became {{jalink|Bettoji|Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1882|Q7812}}), {{jalink|Modern company ranking system|Q712534}} . === God level === * {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{jalink|784th year|Q30083}}) {{jalink|August 3rd|Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{jalink|Shoku Nihongi|Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{jalink|Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 5th year ({{jalink|863|Q25052}}) Leap {{jalink|June 27th|Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{jalink|Nagazuzō|Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri |Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{jalink|6th century|Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{jalink|1911|Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{jalink|Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{jalink|Toyoukebime|Q2983724|Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{jalink|Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|Akiba Shrine}} in {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}}15 ({{jalink|1587|Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{jalink|1971|Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{jalink|Tanakaozuka Tumulus}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Otsu City|Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{jalink|Mio Shrine|Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Shiga Prefecture|Q131358}}{{jalink|Takashima City|Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===
