Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill||lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine Crest|UNKNOWN}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine Crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|1=UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|1=UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine Crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|1=UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=May 3rd|Q2547|WD=Q2547}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Mizuo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Mizuo Shrine'' (Miojinja/Miojinja) is located in Haido, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Divine Crest|1=UNKNOWN}} is "Chrysanthemum" [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency). . === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}}''' *: The 11th {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|WD=Q314850}} prince. In ``Ki|Q813935}}'', he is described as the ancestor of Mio-kun (Mio clan) and Hakui-kun (Hakui clan).{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. *: Deity enshrined at Kanan Shrine (south main shrine: current shrine). * '''Himegami'' *: Refers to {{ill|lt=Shinhime|Q28415396|WD=Q28415396}}, the mother of the 26th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keitai|Q349277|WD=Q349277}}. *: He is the enshrined deity of Kahoku Shrine (North Main Hall), and was transferred to Kanan Shrine in 1971|Q2475}} in 1946 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), where he was enshrined there. As for the enshrined deities, there were once {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}} (Kanansha), {{ill|lt=Ame no Uzume|Q462803|WD=Q462803|lt=Tencho Onna no Mikoto}} (Kahoku There was a theory that there were two deities enshrined at the shrine, {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, but the shrine is said to enshrine the ancestral deity of Mio-kun, and the two deities currently enshrined are Iwakushibetsu-no-Mikoto and Hime-kami. === History === === Founding ===

Mizuo Shrine used to consist of two shrines, Konan-sha (south main shrine) and Kahoku-sha (northern main shrine), which were separated by the Mizuo River, but now it consists of one shrine. The shrine biography The {{ill|lt=auspicious origin|Q522046|WD=Q522046}} of Mizuo Shrine is based on the ``Sanbi Daimyojin Mainland Chronicle'' Mama written by Waniko Yasutoshi, an ascetic monk in the early modern period. In {{Harv|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=354}}, it is said that {{ill|lt=Iwakushibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} came to live in the place where Sarutahiko no Mikoto is enshrined in order to learn Tensei Shinto of {{ill|lt=Sartahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko no Mikoto}}. name="historical book"/>. Since he worshiped Mio Daimyojin (Nagata Shrine in Nagata, Takashima City) in the morning and evening, where Sarutahiko no Mikoto was enshrined, this place was called ``Haido'' (later Haido), and the site of his residence was called ``Haisho Gosho''. Since Iwashibetsu no Mikoto died in this area, his son, Iwaki Betsuo, buried Iwashibetsu no Mikoto on Mt. Mt. Mt. Mt. Mio, and founded Kanan-sha Shrine (present-day shrine) to enshrine Iwashibetsu no Mikoto.Subsequently, according to the shrine's biography, the 11th prince of Emperor Ojin, Hayaso Betsuno, also came to the area to learn Tensei Shinto, and his fourth grandson, {{ill|lt=Hikoshuno|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}}, adopted Furihime, the 5th grandson of Iwaki Betsuno, as his wife. Furihime is said to have given birth to three children at the same time, Amaatobe, Otoatobe, and Taitobe (Oatobe, referring to the 26th Emperor Keitai) at the shrine of Mizuo Shrine. It is said that when the child was born, King Hikoshu built a temporary shrine to pray for a safe delivery, and later, King Tenkabu built Mienari Shrine to enshrine his parents on the site of the temporary shrine, which was the beginning of ``Kahoku Shrine'', and after that, the shrine came to enshrine only the god Hime (Furihime). This Kahoku Shrine collapsed in 1971, so it has now been moved to Kana Shrine (current location). === Outline === The first reference to the document is "{{ill|lt=Shinshōkakuchofusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312}}" "Shinshokakuchofusho" 10 (Shinji Shoke Fudo) dated 1st year of Daido (806). In the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181}} ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|WD=Q31216}}), 13 {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private house)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe}} (Private house attached to a shrine) is provided {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}}3 years ({{ill|lt=784|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}), both of them were given the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Rank, {{jalink|Jōkan|Jōkan} to the Three-Tailed God. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) You can see the article that {{ill|lt=Kankai|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is lower than the Junior Fourth Rank, was conferred {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}.{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}} 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Omi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882}}{{jalink|Takashima County (Shiga Prefecture) | Q11669692 | Takashima District As part of the ``Monthly New Year'', two zas will be placed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{jalink |Ninamesai|Q11501518}} It is stated that it was kept in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. When Zenchi-in was opened in {{ill|lt=Koan|Q389180|WD=Q389180}}3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|WD=Q5536}}), it became {{ill|lt=Bettoji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}} of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. After that, the company did not perform well and its social status declined. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, in the 15th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1882|Q7812|WD=Q7812}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} . === God level === * {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=784th year|Q30083|WD=Q30083}}) {{ill|lt=August 3rd|Q2786|WD=Q2786}}, Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399}}") - Written as "Three-Tailed God." * {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} 5th year ({{ill|lt=863|Q25052|WD=Q25052}}) Leap {{ill|lt=June 27th|Q2664|WD=Q2664}}, from the lower rank of the fifth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - The notation is ``Three-Tailed God.'' === Precincts === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) honden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main shrine/middle gate}}]] The main shrine is a one-ken shrine {{ill|lt=Nagazuzō|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}. In front of the main hall, a worship hall is built across the middle gate. The worship hall is {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}}. Additionally, within the precincts, the Japanese-style garden ``Mizuo Garden'' was completed in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}). In addition, there are remains of {{ill|lt=6th century|Q8090|WD=Q8090}} group of tombs (Haido tumulus group) in the mountains behind the precincts, and among them, Ojizuka is said to be the tomb of the ancestor of the Three-Tailed Prince. File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) teien.JPG|Mio-jinja Garden === Setsusha === [[File:Mio-jinja (Takashima) hachimansha.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Hachimansha}}]] * Hachiman Shrine (three shrines) The description of Setsusha Shrine is based on the Shrine History Book. - The three companies were independent, but in 1911 ({{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|WD=Q2076}}) they were enshrined in one shrine. ** Shinmei Shrine - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoukebime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Omikami}}. ** Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}}, Hassobetsu no Mikoto (Prince of Emperor Ojin). ** Akiba Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fire Endowment}}. Solicited from {{ill|lt=Akibayama Hongu Akiba Shrine|Q500413|WD=Q500413|lt=Akiba Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}}15 ({{ill|lt=1587|Q6744|WD=Q6744}}). === Festival === * Saitan Festival (January 1st)Description of annual festivals is based on the shrine history book. * Sagicho Festival (January 15th) * Prayer Festival (March 16th) * Fire Extinguishment Festival (April 15th) * Annual festival (May 3rd) * Dorochi Festival (early June) * Hundred Lantern Festival (June 26th) * Respect for the Aged Festival, Hachiman Shrine Annual Festival, Memorial Service for the War Dead (September 15th) * Akiba Shrine Annual Festival (September 18th) *Autumn New Year (October 17th) * Niinamesai (November 23rd) === Related locations === * Kitahonden Ruins ** Location: Kamiaido, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|45.78|N|135|59|6.86|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Kitahonden Ruins}}) *: Kahokusha (northern main hall) ruins. It was destroyed by a typhoon in 1971 ({{ill|lt=1971|Q2475|WD=Q2475}}), so it was relocated to Kanan Shrine (currently Mizuo Shrine). A stone monument was erected at the site, but it was later moved so there are no remains [https://web.archive.org/web/20140513010549/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum221201-02.html Mizuo Shrine North Main Hall (Old Site)] (Investigation of Engishiki Shrine ). . * Nagata Shrine ** Location: Nagata, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|18|23.31|N|136|0|25.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Nagata Shrine (Mio Daimyojin)}}) *: Shikinaisha. is said to be the "Mio Daimyojin" that Iwashibetsu no Mikoto worshiped morning and evening. * Otaki Shrine ** Location: Otowa, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|17|59.87|N|136|0|5.15|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Osui Shrine (Osuiden Ruins)}})*: It is said to be the ruins of the large cooking hall of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. It is also said that the large stone at the head of the shrine is the cornerstone of the first torii gate of Mizuo Shrine {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}}. * Ota Shrine ** Location: Aoyagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|0.08|N|136|1|55.57|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Ota Shrine (Otabisho)}}) *: Formerly known as "Shingu Shrine" . It is said that it was a tourist place for Mizuo Shrine, and its distance from Mizuo Shrine indicates its former power.{{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986|p=353-355}} * Miou Shrine ** Location: Tokiwagi, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|38.59|N|136|0|33.14|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Mieio Shrine}}) *: Shikinaisha. The deities enshrined are Hikoshu-o and Furi-hime. The company name ``Mieusei'' is said to be derived from the three children Furihime gave birth to. * Safe delivery leaning stone ** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|18.84|N|136|0|29.06|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Easy delivery leaning stone (Furihime birth legend place)}}) *: Legendary place where Furihime gave birth to her triplet children. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tanakaozuka Tumulus|1=UNKNOWN}} (Azumi Mausoleum reference site)** Location: Tanaka, Azumigawa-cho, Takashima City ({{Coord|35|20|24.57|N|136|0|24.07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|name=Tanakaozuka Tumulus (Azumi Mausoleum reference site, Hikoshu King's tomb tradition site)}}) *: Legendary site of Hikoshu King's Tomb. === Historical evidence === [[File:Mio-jinja (Otsu), shaden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mio Shrine ({{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}})}}]] A shrine that is presumed to be related to Mizuo Shrine is {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Otsu City|Q202907|WD=Q202907}}{{jalink| {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}, which is known as {{ill|lt=Mio Shrine|Q65247874|WD=Q65247874}}, the god of landowners, is located near Onjoji Temple|Q1756307}}. According to ``Koshichi Shiryaku,'' the company enshrines three gods that washed up in Otsu from Miozaki, Takashima District on spiritual materials {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. From this, it has been pointed out that there is a connection with Mizuo Shrine, and it has also been pointed out that the ``Omi Province Three Tailed God'' that appears in historical materials may refer to this place {{Sfn|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358}}{{ill|lt=Takashima City|Q845794|WD=Q845794}}Haido 716 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125}} (JR West Japan) {{ill|lt=Kosei Line|Q599974|WD=Q599974}} {{ill|lt=Omi-Takashima Station|Q5363194|WD=Q5363194}} (about 35 minutes walk) === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * Precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Prefecture Gosha Meiji Shrine Materials|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Materials Editorial Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine Materials|1912}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/131}} Refer to 131 frames of "Meiji Shrine Journal Materials Fuken Gosha Naka"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection). * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596}}|author=Tetsuo Hashimoto|year=1986|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 5 Yamashiro/Omi|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470}}|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=Mizuo Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 25 Place names in Shiga Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|Mizuo Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Iwashibetsu no Mikoto|Q11587884|WD=Q11587884}} * {{ill|lt=Hiko Master King|Q11487892|WD=Q11487892}} * {{ill|lt=Hakui Shrine|Q11610064|WD=Q11610064}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Mio-jinja (Takashima)}} * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/manabu/history01.html Emperor Keitai (Learn from people related to Takashima)] - Takashima City Tourism Association * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1420 Mizuo Shrine] - Shiga Prefectural Shrine Agency * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210101.html Mizuo Shrine Niza] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}}
